title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to increase the amount of the child tax credit, to make such credit fully refundable, to remove income limitations from such credit, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill would increase the child tax credit that families get on their taxes. Instead of getting up to one thousand dollars per child, families would get up to five thousand dollars per child. The bill also removes the income limits, which means all families can get the full credit no matter how much money they make. These changes would start for taxes filed in 2025.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1 Child Tax Credit Improvements
sections.text: This section makes several changes to the child tax credit rules in the tax code. First, it increases the amount of money families can get from one thousand dollars per child to five thousand dollars per child. Second, it removes all income limits, which means families can get this credit no matter how much money they make. The current law stops giving the credit to families who earn too much money, but this bill would let all families get it. Third, it removes several parts of the current law that are no longer needed or that limit who can get the credit. This includes removing special rules about Puerto Rico residents and getting rid of advance payment rules. Fourth, it makes technical fixes to other parts of the tax code to match these changes. All of these changes would start applying to tax years that begin after December thirty first, two thousand twenty four.
